title: EC2 sizing benchmark - OpenQuake hazard
description: OpenQuake classical hazard job used to benchmark EC2 vCPU/family cost vs wall-clock completion time (#344). Full 2022 GMCM logic tree (from NSHM_v1.0.4) x a single SRM branch (srm_single_branch_TEST.json) => one job, 21 gsims. submit_oq_hazard_matrix.py injects submission_arg_overrides (ecs_vcpu, ecs_memory) per cell; the job runs to completion so wall time is the metric. Sites are the NZ 0.2 deg grid (NZ_0_2_NB_1_1, ~1057) - a ~4x bump up from the original SRWG214+NZ (~250) so that fixed serial overhead (OQ startup, source-model build, export/store) stays a small fraction of wall time even in the high-vCPU cells and doesn't flatten the scaling curve. A 4-vCPU pilot at 250 sites ran ~1521s; ~1057 sites lands well under the 240-min job limit. Tune imts/locations from a --limit 1 pilot if the run is too short/long or a low-memory (c-family) cell OOMs.
